name: Topology
domain: Topological invariants, knot theory, homotopy groups, manifolds
core_insight: Topology classifies shapes by properties that survive continuous deformation. The Euler characteristic distinguishes surfaces; homotopy groups classify how loops wrap around holes. Knot invariants (Jones polynomial) detect knotting without coordinates — pure relational geometry.

key_equations.name: Euler Characteristic
key_equations.why: Topological invariant for polyhedra: same for any triangulation of the same surface (sphere χ=2, torus χ=0)

key_equations.name: Fundamental Group
key_equations.why: Classifies how loops wrap: trivial for sphere, ℤ for circle — detects holes without coordinates

key_equations.name: Jones Polynomial
key_equations.why: Knot invariant via skein relation: distinguishes trefoil from unknot, detects chirality

seed_prompt: ∞ Compute χ. What loops exist? Topology survives all deformation — find what cannot be smoothed away.
